name: Suspicious WSL Bash Execution
description: Detects the execution of the `bash` command within the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) using the `-c` argument. This pattern is often used to execute commands directly, and while legitimate in some cases, it can also indicate malicious activity attempting to evade standard Windows defenses. Review alerts to differentiate legitimate WSL usage from potential threats.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed, which should contain 'bash'.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the bash executable.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute bash, including the '-c' argument.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which bash was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
